text:
There were selfies, iconic poses and athletes in a state of "pure happiness" in Paris after Australia won two more silver medals and another bronze at the Olympic Games.

Australia is closing in on 50 medals at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games after adding two silvers and another bronze to the tally overnight.

Podium finishes were recorded in track cycling, diving and canoe sprint in the French capital, taking Australia's total medal haul to 48.

Australia maintained its third place on the medal table, with two more days to go in what is already the nation's most successful Olympics with 18 golds.

The country's first diving medal of the games came on Thursday when Maddi Keeney claimed silver in the women's 3m springboard.

Keeney later posed for a set of iconic photos with China's Chen Yiwen (gold medallist) and Chang Yani (bronze medallist).

On social media, Keeney posted a pictures with the caption "pure happiness", while also adding a photo to her Instagram story with the remark: "Is this real life".

In track cycling, Matthew Richardson rode to Australia's 50th track cycling medal in Olympic history, and his second of the Paris games.

Richardson took home the silver medal in the men's sprint, behind Harrie Lavreysen of the Netherlands.

Earlier in the week, he claimed bronze alongside Matthew Glaetzer and Leigh Hoffman in the men's team sprint - Australia's first medal in that event since Sydney 2000.

On social media, Richardson posted a photo of his silver and bronze medals side-by-side with the caption: "Trying to collect them all", noting he still had the keirin to go.

In the men's canoeing K-2 500m final, Australians Tom Green and Jean van der Westhuyzen finished with a bronze medal.

"Winning an Olympic medal is so special. We're so stoked with that," van der Westhuyzen said.

Elsewhere in Paris, the Opals hopes of playing for the gold medal were dashed after a 85-64 loss to the United States in the women's basketball semi-final.

"We had to play a perfect game today. We came out really flat. We had too many turnovers and shot selection wasn't great," coach Sandy Brondello said.

The Opals still have a chance at a medal, with the bronze medal match scheduled for 7:30pm AEST on Sunday.

In beach volleyball, Tokyo 2020 silver medallists Mariafe Artacho del Solar and Taliqua Clancy missed out on the podium after a heartbreaking loss in the bronze medal match against Nina Brunner and Tanja Hueberli of Switzerland.

"It definitely stings, for sure at the moment and probably will for a really long time," Clancy said.

"I'm sure this game will be haunting me but also the positive of that is that it will definitely keep the fire burning."
